commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=abdb93696409c410b2f4447af002fb981e095f84)
commands.review_summary:
The PR reroutes Telegram default text sends, edits, outbound chunking, bot delivery, and draft previews from raw rich-message calls back to sendMessage/editMessageText with HTML/plain fallback and updated tests.

PR surface: Source +196, Tests -67. Total +129 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source/report level: current main still routes Telegram final text and draft previews through rich-message paths, and the linked issue has cross-client unsupported-message evidence. I did not run a live Telegram repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor/maintainer follow-up is needed because exact-head checks fail and live proof only covers final replies, not draft-preview edits.

target.title: fix(telegram): restore readable default text sends
target.body: ## Summary Fixes #93263 by restoring Telegram's default text delivery, edits, and draft previews to the legacy-readable `sendMessage` / `editMessageText` path instead of the rich raw Bot API path. This keeps the change scoped to the outage path: - default text sends now render HTML and fall back to plain text when Telegram rejects formatting - draft previews now use normal text send/edit calls again - Telegram default text chunk limits are pulled back to the standard text-message range instead of the rich-message limit - media delivery, buttons, quote handling, and related transport behavior stay in place ## Linked context Closes #93263 Related #93279 review threads asking for exact-head typecheck cleanup and real Telegram proof. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior addressed: Telegram default outbound replies and preview edits became less readable after the rich-message rollout; this patch restores readable default text replies and draft preview editing on the normal `sendMessage` / `editMessageText` path. - Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway on macOS with the bundled Telegram channel configured against a real Telegram bot and a real direct-message chat. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: 1. Restarted the local OpenClaw gateway through Telegram (`/restart`). 2. Sent `Hello openclaw` and `你好` to the Telegram bot from a real Telegram client. 3. Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`. 4. Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/telegram-outbound.test.ts`. - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Telegram client screenshot captured at `~/Desktop/Screenshot_20260615_224215.jpg` shows readable bot replies for both `Hello openclaw` and `你好`. Copied live output from the real chat: user `Hello openclaw` -> bot `嗨！有什么需要帮忙的吗？`; user `你好` -> bot `你好！有什么需要帮忙的吗？`. - Observed result after fix: The Telegram bot delivered readable plain chat replies in the real DM instead of raw/unfriendly rich payload output, and the targeted outbound-path regression tests passed on the PR head. - What was not tested: I did not capture a separate live screenshot/video of draft preview streaming in Telegram in this pass; live proof here covers readable reply delivery, while preview-edit behavior is covered by the targeted Telegram draft/outbound test suite on this head. - Proof limitations or environment constraints: Real Telegram proof is limited to one-owner DM traffic against a local gateway and a single bot account. The screenshot contains only non-secret bot/chat content; bot tokens and private config values were not included. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, the PR thread and issue report described Telegram default text output regressing onto the rich-message path and producing unreadable/default-hostile delivery. ## Tests and validation Passed: - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/telegram-outbound.test.ts` Fixed on exact PR head: - `extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.replies.ts` TypeScript reference updated to use the local chunk resolver type instead of the non-exported `TelegramTextChunk` alias. - `extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts` mock-call assertions tightened so `tsgo` no longer infers an empty tuple element access. Not run in this push: - full `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` after the user asked to stop the long-running queued `tsgo` pass and push after the targeted Telegram tests - autoreview ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? Yes Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No What is the highest-risk area? Telegram default text delivery and draft preview editing paths. How is that risk mitigated? The change stays inside the Telegram delivery/draft path, keeps media/button/thread handling intact, and is covered by targeted Telegram send, draft-stream, and outbound contract tests plus a real DM proof. ## Current review state What is the next action? Maintainer review on the updated exact-head patch and proof. What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? If reviewers want separate live preview-stream evidence beyond the real DM reply proof, I can add a follow-up artifact from the same Telegram bot setup. Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? - Fixed the exact-head TypeScript failures called out in review. - Added real Telegram proof in the PR body using the required proof-field format.
target.author: nianjiuzst
